series_metadata.series_description: Mistaken Identity files are created when a lien is mistakenly put on a taxpayer with the same name as another. The taxpayer will provide information about the error and Collections Enforcement will review the issue and cross check CUBS for any hits to determine whether or not the lien was put on the proper person. If it is mistaken identity, an affidavit is needed to prove the mistaken identity. Records include, but are not limited to, the original report of mistaken identity, an affidavit, and, if determined a mistaken identity, a notification about the error to be sent to the Clerk of Courts.
